info.title: REST Services
info.description: Skubana (now Extensiv) REST API for inventory, order, product, shipment, and warehouse management.

post.description:
Adjust product stock quantities, will override existing quantities.

This endpoint should be used carefully as it might override pending activity. It is recommended to use /inventory/add and /inventory/remove instead.

get.description: Returns a list of custom field definitions for order items. Use 'names' for exact match on multiple names, or 'name' for partial match (contains) on a single name.

parameters.description: Filter by partial name match (contains). Ignored if 'names' is provided.

parameters.name: names
parameters.description: Filter by exact name match from a list. Takes precedence over 'name' parameter.

post.description: Update fields for order items in an order. Only certain sales channel types support edits on quantity, unit price, and discount. This endpoint allows only one request at a time. A new request will not be accepted until the previous one finishes

put.description: Add order items. Only certain sales channel types support adding items. This endpoint allows only one request at a time. A new request will not be accepted until the previous one finishes

delete.description: Delete order items. Only certain sales channel types support deleting order items. This endpoint allows only one request at a time. A new request will not be accepted until the previous one finishes

get.summary: Retrieve product current costs by warehouse
get.description:
Retrieve product current costs by warehouse.

costType has 3 possible values:

  1. PO_FIFO_QUEUE: This is the main costType; it has precedence over the 2 other ones. Every time an order is shipped, a unit is deducted from the oldest purchase order with available units. Once there are no more units available from the PO_FIFO_QUEUE costType, The cost will be fetched from the VENDOR_PRODUCT costType.

  2. VENDOR_PRODUCT: This is the cost coming from the oldest default vendor product's pricing tiers.

  3. DEFAULT_VENDOR_COST: This is the last resort costType; if no PO_FIFO_QUEUE and no VENDOR_PRODUCT costTypes are present the cost will be fetched from DEFAULT_VENDOR_COST

200.description: Successful retrieval of the current product cost

404.description: No warehouse found with the given id

post.description: Transfer inventory quantities from a location to an other

get.description:
Provides information about the inventory and the inventory’s value that the company has currently. Each inventory value entry comes with additional information about the origin of the inventory and a breakdown of how it’s calculated in case it’s a kit-bundle built from components. The additional information can include: Purchase orders, vendors, inventory reconciliation, inventory transfers etc.

Search requirements and rules:

  • Either productIds or warehouseIds need to be specified in the request.

  • minAvailable cannot be greater than maxAvailable

CostSourceTypes options and definitions:

  • UNDEFINED: The origin of this inventory value is undefined

  • COGS_FIFO_QUEUE: The origin of this inventory value is purchase orders

  • VENDOR_PRODUCT: There is no origin of this inventory value and the value was picked from the product -> vendor product pricing tiers

  • DEFAULT_PRODUCT_COST: There is no origin of this inventory value and the value was picked from the product -> vendor cost field

  • INVENTORY_ADDITION: The origin of this inventory value is inventory reconciliation (when adding inventory to reconcile inventory)

  • ROLL_UP_FROM_BREAKDOWN: The origin of this inventory value is when kit-bundle inventory value is calculated based on its components

Common use cases and examples:

  • Find all available inventory value for a warehouse: Search params: ?warehouseId=yourWarehouseId&minAvailable=1&emptyInventoryTransferSourceWarehouse=true

  • Find all available inventory value for a product across all warehouse: Search params: ?productId=yourProductId&minAvailable=1&emptyInventoryTransferSourceWarehouse=true

  • Find all in transit inventory value from warehouse A o to warehouse B: Search params: ?warehouseId=warehouseBId&inventoryTransferSourceWarehouseIds=warehouseAId&minAvailable=1

  • Glossary

    • Available Inventory: inventory that has not been released on orders or inventory transfers.

    • In transit inventory: Inventory being transferred from one warehouse to another warehouse.

      • How to identify In transit inventory value entries?

        • available quantity is greater than zero:

          • warehouse and inventoryTransferSourceWarehouse are different than null.

          • warehouse id and inventoryTransferSourceWarehouse id are not equal.

parameters.name: emptyInventoryTransferSourceWarehouse
parameters.description: set emptyInventoryTransferSourceWarehouse to true to exclude inventory transfers from your search. To exclude all inventory transfers, inventoryTransferSourceWarehouseIds must be empty

parameters.name: inventoryTransferSourceWarehouseIds
parameters.description: use inventoryTransferSourceWarehouseIds to search inventory value involved in inventory transfers

parameters.name: maxAvailable
parameters.description: set maxAvailable to 0 to search inventory value entries that have been already released on orders or inventory transfers

parameters.name: minAvailable
parameters.description: set minAvailable to 1 to search inventory value entries that have not been already released on orders or inventory transfers yet

get.description:
Provides information about COGS (cost of goods sold) for inventory and inventory value that has been released on orders (fulfilled orders). Each cogs entry comes with additional information like order, product and listing information and about the origin of the inventory including a breakdown of how it’s calculated in case it’s a kit-bundle built from components. The additional information can include: Purchase orders, vendors, inventory reconciliation, inventory transfers etc.

Search requirements and rules:

  • At least one of the following search parameters is required:

    • productIds, warehouseIds, listingIds, orderItemIds, orderIds or inventoryValueIds

  • minQuantity cannot be greater than maxQuantity

CostSourceTypes options and definitions:

  • UNDEFINED: The origin of this inventory value is undefined

  • COGS_FIFO_QUEUE: The origin of this inventory value is purchase orders

  • VENDOR_PRODUCT: There is no origin of this inventory value and the value was picked from the product -> vendor product pricing tiers

  • DEFAULT_PRODUCT_COST: There is no origin of this inventory value and the value was picked from the product -> vendor cost field

  • INVENTORY_ADDITION: The origin of this inventory value is inventory reconciliation (when adding inventory to reconcile inventory)

  • ROLL_UP_FROM_BREAKDOWN: The origin of this inventory value is when kit-bundle inventory value is calculated based on its components

Common use cases and examples:

  • Find all cogs for orders fulfilled from for a warehouse A and warehouse B: Search params: ?warehouseIds=warehousAId,warehouseBId&minQuantity=1

  • Find all cogs for orders fulfilled for a listing: Search params: ?listingIds=listingId&minQuantity=1

  • Find all cogs for orders fulfilled for an order: Search params: ?orderIds=orderId&minQuantity=1

  • Find all cogs for orders fulfilled for an order item: Search params: ?orderItemIds=orderId&minQuantity=1

  • Find all cogs associated with an inventory value entry: Search params: ?inventoryValueIds=inventoryValueId&minQuantity=1

  • Find all cogs associated with a product that originated from default costs: Search params: ?productIds=productId&minQuantity=1&emptyInventoryValue=true

  • Find all reinserted cogs associated with a product caused by voided shipments: Search params: ?productIds=productId&maxQuantity=0

  • Glossary

    • Reinserted COGS: Inventory value that has been reintroduced into a warehouse that was used previously as COGS for an order but the shipment was voided and the inventory has been reinserted.

    • Default costs: COGS calculated based on default vendor product costs and product vendor cost fields. Default costs are used when Purchase orders or/and Inventory reconciliation features are not used.

parameters.name: emptyInventoryValue
parameters.description: set emptyInventoryValue to true to search cogs that originate from default costs

parameters.name: maxQuantity
parameters.description: set maxQuantity to 0 to search only for reinserted cogs caused by shipments voided

parameters.name: minQuantity
parameters.description: set minQuantity to 1 to exclude reinserted cogs caused by shipments voided

inventoryTransferSourceWarehouse.description: Only used for Inventory Transfer Orders. Determines the origin/source of the inventory transfer shipment

warehouse.description: Warehouse where the inventory is located. In case of inventory transfer shipments, this is the receiving warehouse

reached.description: If updating, reached is required. If only creating, it will default to false if not provided

milestoneId.description: If provided, will attempt to update, otherwise will attempt to create

receiveByDate.description: Latest date recipient can receive order

shipByDate.description: Latest date seller can ship order

shipByDate.description: Latest date seller can ship order

productIds.description: If specified, this will assign the category to these products

masterSku.description: Mandatory if productId is not set

productId.description: Mandatory if masterSku is not set

stockLocationName.description: Location of the stock. Mandatory for 'DIRECT FULFILLMENT' warehouses. Default as GLOBAL for non-'DIRECT_FULFILLMENT' warehouses.

customFieldValues.description: There is a maximum of 25 custom fields per order.

customShipBillingOption.description: Where to bill shipping, default is BILL_SHIPPING_TO_MY_ACCOUNT

externalOrderFulfillmentWarehouseId.description: This option is only available for external orders on channels with external fulfillment sources.

orderNumber.description: max length = 191, if not set an order number will be generated automatically

paymentDate.description: If unset the order will go to 'AWAITING_PAYMENT' status

receiveByDate.description: Latest date recipient can receive order

shipState.description: max length = 20, mandatory if shipCountry is USA

listingName.description: Has no impact on what corresponding listing and product is attached to this order item. That is done through listingSku value.

listingSku.description: If this does not equal an existing listing sku for the order's sales channel, order will move to unresolved status until proper listing is assigned

carrierFee.description: Will be ignored and generated from the shipping provider if 'generateShippingLabels' is set to true.

forwardToEmail.description: If specified, and if 'generateShippingLabels' is set to true, the return label will be emailed to this email address.

generateShippingLabels.description: Set whether to get shipping labels from the provider for this RMA.

trackingNumber.description: Will be ignored and generated from the shipping provider if 'generateShippingLabels' is set to true.

transactionId.description: Will be ignored and generated from the shipping provider if 'generateShippingLabels' is set to true.

packageTypeId.description: Required if 'generateShippingLabels' is set to true.

shippingCarrier.description: Required if 'generateShippingLabels' is set to false.

shippingProviderId.description: Required if 'generateShippingLabels' is set to true.

shippingServiceId.description: Required if 'generateShippingLabels' is set to true.

defaultIncotermShippingRule.description: defaultIncotermShippingRule is deprecated, use defaultIncotermShippingRuleId; defaultIncotermShippingRule and defaultIncotermShippingRuleId are mutually exclusive

defaultIncotermShippingRuleId.description: defaultIncotermShippingRuleId and defaultIncotermShippingRule are mutually exclusive

shippingProviderId.description: Either a shippingProviderId or shippingCarrier is required.
